channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SAS soldier involved in war crimes allegations goes on reality TV after alleged prisoner execution
item.description: Former SAS soldier Henry Jones has become a key witness in the murder case against Ben Roberts-Smith, after being accused of executing a prisoner during a 2012 mission in Afghanistan. Jones, who has a...

item.title: Assessment of Climate Commitments in Tamil Nadu Election Manifestos
item.description: As Tamil Nadu heads into elections, climate change has emerged as a pressing and immediate concern rather than a distant environmental issue. The State is already witnessing the impact of rising tempe...

item.title: Independent Review of Auspokies Highlights Strengths in Casino Ratings and Game Insights
item.description: This sponsored article takes a close look at Auspokies, a platform wey dey provide reviews and information about Aussie pokies and online casinos. The write-up tries to answer whether the site truly d...

item.title: Pope Leo Concludes 11-Day Africa Tour with Open-Air Mass in Equatorial Guinea
item.description: Pope Leo XIV don waka go four different African countries for 11 days, travel 18,000 kilometres and perform eight masses. Dis journey na im first major international tour as US pontiff. During di trip...

item.title: Dingoes Attack Three Children at Karijini National Park, Rangers Seek to Remove Threat
item.description: At least three children have been attacked by dingoes at a remote campground in Karijini National Park, Western Australia. The most serious incident occurred when a four-year-old girl was bitten on th...

item.title: Tesla ramps up capital spending as it shifts toward AI and new factories
item.description: Tesla is entering a period of exceptionally high capital expenditure as it seeks to transform its business beyond electric vehicles. The company has signaled plans to more than double its capital spen...

item.title: Scientists Identify Key Brain Cells Linked to Depression
item.description: Scientists at McGill University and the Douglas Institute have made a groundbreaking discovery by pinpointing the specific brain cells that behave differently in people with depression. Their study, p...

item.title: Three Laguna police chiefs removed over failure to curb illegal gambling
item.description: Tatlong police chiefs sa Laguna ang tinanggal sa kanilang puwesto dahil sa hindi sapat na performance, lalo na sa pagpigil ng illegal gambling sa kanilang mga nasasakupan. Kinumpirma ito ng Calabarzon...

item.title: Artistic Illustrations of Slovenia’s Insects by Marko Rop Blend Science and Playful Design
item.description: The article highlights a beautifully crafted illustration series by Slovenian artist Marko Rop, who depicts the diverse insect life found in his home country. The collection includes a wide range of s...

item.title: US deploys Ukrainian counter-drone system at Saudi base after repeated Iran-linked attacks
item.description: Jeshi la Marekani limeanza kutumia teknolojia ya Ukraine ya kupambana na drone katika kambi muhimu ya anga ya Prince Sultan nchini Saudi Arabia, baada ya mashambulizi ya mara kwa mara yanayohusishwa n...

item.title: Changes to Canadian Citizenship Laws Lead to Increased Interest from American Applicants
item.description: Since December 15, 2025, new Canadian legislation (Bill C-3) has expanded citizenship by descent, allowing individuals to claim Canadian citizenship through grandparents, great-grandparents, and even ...

item.title: Minimally invasive intestinal procedure may help prevent weight regain after stopping GLP-1 weight loss drugs
item.description: A new clinical study suggests that a minimally invasive intestinal procedure called duodenal mucosal resurfacing could help patients maintain weight loss after stopping popular GLP-1 medications such ...

item.title: Nairobi hosts regional anti-corruption summit focused on cross-border corruption and illicit financial flows
item.description: Kenya is currently hosting the 16th Eastern Africa Association of Anti-Corruption Authorities (EAAACA) conference in Nairobi, bringing together key regional and international stakeholders to strengthe...

item.title: Dodoma City Hospital helps reduce patient congestion and improves healthcare services
item.description: The new Dodoma City Hospital, which started operations in 2023, is helping reduce the heavy patient load at Dodoma Regional Hospital and Makole Health Centre. The facility, constructed with over Sh2.7...

item.title: PM Modi addresses rally in West Bengal, criticises Trinamool Congress and references 'Jhalmuri' remark
item.description: Based on the available headline, the news article refers to a political statement made by Prime Minister Narendra Modi during an event or rally in West Bengal. In his speech, he appears to have used a...

item.title: Delhi CM inspects Aruna Asaf Ali Hospital, directs measures for summer preparedness
item.description: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta carried out an unannounced visit to Aruna Asaf Ali Hospital on Thursday to review the hospital’s readiness and services. During her inspection, she spoke with doctors ...

item.title: Meru Woman Fulfils Lifelong Dream by Building Fully Furnished 3-Bedroom Home for Her Mother
item.description: A young woman from Meru County has touched many after fulfilling a personal dream of improving her family’s living conditions by building her mother a fully furnished three-bedroom bungalow. Winnie sh...

item.title: Tanzanian golfer Madina Idd plans increased participation in WAGR tournaments to climb global rankings
item.description: Tanzanian golfer Madina Idd has shared her clear ambition to take part in more World Amateur Golf Ranking (WAGR) tournaments in order to improve her position on the global amateur golf leaderboard. Sp...

item.title: Nine individuals face charges over hazing incident at PNPA
item.description: Nine individuals, including seven cadets and two police officers, are facing criminal charges over the hazing of 22 plebes at the Philippine National Police Academy (PNPA). The charges were filed unde...

item.title: Global Summit of 53 Nations to Discuss Phasing Out Fossil Fuels and Transition to Renewable Energy
item.description: In a historic move, over 50 nations are meeting in Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss strategies for phasing out fossil fuels such as coal, oil, and gas. This summit, originally planned before the esca...
